title: Astylus alpinus
intro:
There is no information available on Astylus alpinus. However, I can give you some information about a plant that shares a similar name, Astragalus alpinus, commonly known as Alpine Milkvetch, or Astragalus.

Astragalus alpinus is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Western Himalayas, belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being, with reported health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus alpinus include saponins, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Astragalus alpinus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine to tonify the body and enhance vitality.
